refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency percentile and network conditions to clarify acceptance criteria and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency over the network link during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms under defined network conditions.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link performance to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low processing delay.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized communication protocols with low overhead and prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to preprocess and reduce data volume before transmission.
DP3: Employ dedicated network link with QoS guarantees and latency monitoring.
DP4: Design telemetry and tasking interfaces with asynchronous buffering and timestamping.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized communication protocols with low overhead and prioritization for teleoperation data.

potential_sources: Interaction between telemetry and tasking data streams
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication protocol optimization, minimizing coupling with other DPs by focusing on data prioritization and overhead reduction.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed baseline parameters.
Risk of protocol overhead causing latency spikes under peak load.
Assumes telemetry and tasking data can be prioritized without loss of critical information.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP2) if latency targets are not met with protocol optimization alone.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication protocols, data prioritization, and latency management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection carrying teleoperation data with latency constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Input interface receiving telemetry data from Vehicle for transmission.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Output interface sending operator commands to Vehicle.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of DP1 logic within Teleoperation module; low diffusion as communication protocol logic is centralized.
Vehicle module handles telemetry and tasking endpoints but not protocol optimization; moderate diffusion risk if protocol logic is duplicated.
Minimal relevance to communication latency DP.
System-level coordination but no direct implementation of communication protocol.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing communication protocol optimization in the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact if Teleoperation module interfaces are not tightly integrated with Vehicle telemetry/tasking endpoints.
Risk of duplicated protocol logic if Vehicle module implements partial communication handling.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface contracts and avoid protocol logic duplication between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (optimized communication protocols) directly addresses the refined FR of minimizing latency with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Potential duplication of protocol logic in Vehicle module if not carefully managed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment of DP logic with low diffusion risk if interfaces are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end across the Teleoperation module interfaces using network monitoring tools.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of communication protocol DP to Teleoperation IM with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Define strict interface contracts between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to prevent protocol logic duplication.
Consider monitoring tools integration within Teleoperation module for latency verification.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, risks related to interface clarity and potential logic duplication require refinement before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link performance to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low processing delay.

revise.why: To clarify interface contracts and ownership boundaries between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to mitigate diffusion risks.

assumptions: Network conditions meet agreed baseline parameters.
Telemetry and tasking data can be prioritized without loss.
open_risks: Protocol logic duplication between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
Latency spikes under peak load conditions.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under representative network conditions.
Interface performance and integration test results.
